opp.ais_why:
• Customer already runs two active pilots involving MS Copilot for internal solutions, signaling real commitment to AI-driven optimization.
• Stated needs include enhanced AI-driven optimization as an ongoing priority and embedding AI/automation in manual RFP, bidding, and evaluation workflows.
• Next step is to engage Alberto Kunstmann to gauge interest in ANA and Intake. Both modules explicitly named for follow-up.

items.quote: Sandra Montiel (VP Supply Chain, listed as Decision Maker contact role) has never appeared on a call, task, or Next_Step note across the 8-month deal history. Alberto reports to her but she has not engaged.
Alberto articulated qualitative pain (manual RFP workflows, supplier mgmt gaps) but provided no cost-per-cycle, headcount, error rate, cycle time, or ROI target. No quantified value case exists.
No committee structure, approval gates, or timeline discussed. Alberto referenced budget clarity 'early June' but never confirmed outcome. Process is completely unmapped.

sources: Avoma discovery call 29 Oct 2025, Alberto Kunstmann: 'Need to develop supplier relationship management, including onboarding and risk mapping'; 'Current RFP and bidding workflows remain largely manual and require automation'; 'Desire to embed agentic AI in manual RFP and bidding processes to reduce repetitive tasks'
metrics.narrative: Alberto Kunstmann articulated operational pain on 29 Oct 2025: supplier relationship management gaps (onboarding, risk mapping, development) and highly manual RFP/bidding workflows in their current Sabariva tool requiring repetitive evaluation and comparison work. He stated 'we need to develop better' supplier capabilities and expressed desire to 'embed agentic AI in manual RFP and bidding processes to reduce repetitive tasks.' However, NO quantified business case was provided—no cost per RFP cycle, no headcount devoted to manual bid analysis, no error rate, no cycle-time baseline, and no projected ROI or savings target. The pain is confirmed and real (source: Avoma discovery 29 Oct 2025, speaker Alberto Kunstmann), but remains qualitative. To advance, Zycus must co-build a quantified value case with Sierra Gorda finance, anchoring savings to current manual-process cost and cycle time.

sources: Avoma 29 Oct 2025, Alberto requested follow-up demo
Avoma manifest: 5 Nov demo cancelled, 13 Nov no buyer attendance
Next_Step__c: three unanswered follow-ups across Feb-May 2026
OpportunityContactRole: Alberto = Evaluator; Sandra Montiel = Decision Maker but zero engagement
champion.narrative: Alberto Kunstmann was the sole engaged contact and showed initial openness and interest on 29 Oct 2025, requesting a follow-up demo with a solution consultant and proposing specific dates (5 Nov or 6 Nov 2025). However, he is an Evaluator (Supply Chain Excellence Superintendent), NOT a power buyer, and he has NO demonstrated access to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker (his manager Sandra Montiel has never engaged). He cancelled the 5 Nov demo, did not join the 6 Nov session, and has gone completely silent for 225 days despite three rep follow-up attempts (13 Feb, 1 Apr, 22 May 2026 per Next_Step__c). There is no evidence of advocacy—no introductions, no internal championing, no pushing Zycus forward. Alberto is best characterized as a 'cooling contact' rather than a champion: he expressed interest, then disengaged entirely. Champion strength = weak and at high risk of total fade. Re-engagement must determine whether Alberto has budget authority to proceed, whether Sandra (the real power) is aware of and supportive of this initiative, and whether another stakeholder (e.g., Rodrigo Ledezma for IT/digital transformation alignment) must be activated.

sources: Avoma 29 Oct 2025, Alberto: 'today use Sabariva to do our business and negotiation and all the stage of RFP'; 'open to investigate new features to replace what we have today'; 'implementing Microsoft Power Platform and piloting Copilot'; 'we are developing some solutions internally'
Avoma 29 Oct, Kevin: 'Zycus is like a competitor of Sabariva, Coupa, Jaeger'; Alberto: 'Yes'
competition.narrative: Alberto stated on 29 Oct 2025 that Sierra Gorda currently uses Sabariva for RFP/bidding workflows and is building custom procurement automation on Microsoft Power Platform with Copilot pilots for internal processes. He explicitly mentioned evaluating whether Zycus could 'replace what we have today,' framing Sabariva as the incumbent to displace and the DIY Power Platform approach as an internal alternative/competitor (build vs. buy). He also referenced SAP Ariba, Coupa, and Jaeger as 'competitors' when Kevin explained Zycus's position ('Yes, like a competitor of Sabariva, Coupa, Jaeger'), confirming he is aware of the broader market but providing no evidence Sierra Gorda is actively evaluating those vendors. The competitive picture is therefore: (1) Sabariva = incumbent for RFP/sourcing, the direct displacement target; (2) Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot = internal build path, a 'do it ourselves' alternative that may be attractive given their heavy Microsoft investment and lower perceived cost; (3) do-nothing / status quo = highest current threat given 225 days of silence. No other vendor has been named in calls, tasks, or fields. The wedge is Merlin agentic AI for bid evaluation and supplier risk/onboarding automation, capabilities Alberto explicitly said he wants and that Power Platform/Sabariva cannot deliver at enterprise scale—but Zycus has not yet made that case in a live demo or ROI workshop, so the wedge is theoretical, not activated.

sources: Avoma 29 Oct 2025, Alberto Kunstmann: 'Multiple procurement tools in use leading to tool sprawl'; 'Current RFP and quotation comparison process handled manually in Sabariva tool'; 'Open to exploring new features to replace manual bidding and evaluation workflows'; 'Need to develop supplier relationship management, including onboarding and risk mapping'
identify_pain.narrative: Alberto Kunstmann clearly owns and articulated the operational pain on 29 Oct 2025. He described tool sprawl ('maybe too many tools'), manual RFP/bidding workflows in Sabariva requiring repetitive quotation comparison and bid evaluation, and supplier relationship management gaps (no formalized onboarding, no risk mapping, weak development process). He stated: 'today it's very manual, and we are open to investigate new features to replace what we have today' and 'we need to develop better' supplier capabilities. Pain owner is Alberto (Supply Chain Excellence Superintendent), who oversees business improvement, supplier development, and contract control and reports to VP Sandra Montiel. The pain is real and confirmed, but it has not been escalated to the VP level or translated into a board-level compelling event. The risk is that this remains an evaluator-level exploration rather than a C-suite-sponsored transformation, which may explain the 225-day silence.

sources: Avoma 29 Oct 2025: Supply Net portal mentioned for existing contract management
No legal, InfoSec, or SOW discussion in any call, task, or Next_Step
paper_process.narrative: Contracting, legal, procurement channel, InfoSec review, and implementation scoping have not been discussed. No MSA, SOW, or security-questionnaire conversation has occurred. Alberto mentioned Sierra Gorda uses a Chilean supplier portal (Supply Net) for contract lifecycle notifications, implying a procurement-process layer exists, but its interaction with a Zycus SaaS contract is undefined. No SI or partner involvement has been raised. The paper process is a complete unknown and must be surfaced in re-engagement: does Sierra Gorda require a local entity, what is their InfoSec/IT approval cycle (Rodrigo Ledezma, IT and Digital Transformation Manager, is a known contact but has never engaged), and what is the standard contract term and commercialization path for SaaS tools in their procurement stack.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Sandra Montiel (VP Supply Chain, Decision Maker role) but zero call/task evidence
Avoma 29 Oct 2025, Alberto: 'I report to the supply chain vice president'
Next_Step__c: 1 Apr and 22 May 2026 budget check-ins, no buyer response
economic_buyer.narrative: Sandra Montiel (VP Supply Chain) is listed as Decision Maker contact role, but she has NEVER appeared on a recorded call, in any task, or in any Next_Step note across the entire 8-month deal history. Alberto Kunstmann (Supply Chain Excellence Superintendent, an Evaluator-level role reporting TO Sandra per the 29 Oct call: 'I report to the supply chain vice president') has been the sole Zycus contact. No commercial, pricing, or budget conversation has occurred—the two calls were purely discovery-scoping sessions with no dollar discussion. Alberto deferred budget visibility twice: on 1 Apr 2026 he said he 'should know more about Procurement budget commitments' the week of 6 Apr, and on 22 May he indicated he would 'know where budgets stand in early June' (per Next_Step__c)—but Kevin never received a reply. The Economic Buyer is unmapped and unengaged; the deal cannot progress without direct EB access and a commercial conversation anchored to their FY budget cycle.

sources: Avoma manifest: 5 Nov demo cancelled, 13 Nov was Zycus-internal placeholder with no buyer
Next_Step__c: three rep follow-ups (13 Feb, 1 Apr, 22 May 2026) with no buyer reply
No committee, no approval-chain discussion across any call or field
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is entirely unknown. Only Alberto (an Evaluator) has engaged; the VP (Sandra Montiel) has never joined a call. No approval chain, no committee structure, no timeline beyond the placeholder close date has been discussed. Alberto referenced needing to know 'where budgets stand' by early June 2026, implying a budget-allocation gate, but he never confirmed the outcome or the next step. The scheduled follow-up demo (5 Nov 2025 per the 29 Oct call commitment) was cancelled (6 Nov meeting shows 'cancelled' status), and the 13 Nov placeholder call was an internal Zycus check-in with no buyer participation. Since 13 Nov 2025 (225 days ago), the buyer has gone completely silent despite three rep follow-up attempts. The process cannot be mapped because the buyer has disengaged; re-engagement must surface who else must be involved, what internal approval Sierra Gorda requires, and whether this is a 2026 budgeted initiative or a 2027 exploratory effort.

sources: Avoma 29 Oct 2025: 'Need to develop supplier relationship management'; 'embed agentic AI in manual RFP and bidding processes'; 'JD Edwards ERP, Microsoft Power Platform, Supply Net portal, Power BI'; 'Procurement tools will be integrated into Microsoft Teams'
Kevin stated intent to 'help frame the RFP' before formalization (Avoma 13 Nov 2025)
decision_criteria.narrative: Alberto named two explicit capability gaps on 29 Oct 2025 that frame the evaluation: (1) supplier relationship management (onboarding, risk mapping, development), and (2) automation of manual RFP/quotation workflows currently done in Sabariva. He emphasized interest in 'agentic AI' to replace repetitive bid evaluation tasks and stated willingness to 'investigate new features to do a business case if we are able to replace what we have today.' Integration requirements surfaced: JD Edwards ERP, Microsoft Power Platform (piloting Copilot internally), Supply Net portal (Contratos for contract lifecycle), and a mandatory Microsoft Teams embedding (they are migrating from Webex). However, NO formal RFP, BRD, or scorecard has been issued. The criteria remain conversational and unweighted. To confirm, Zycus must surface Sierra Gorda's scoring methodology, must-have vs. nice-to-have features, and any AI/Merlin evaluation benchmarks before a formal RFP is released.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Alberto Kunstmann articulated strong AI appetite on 29 Oct 2025: 'Desire to embed agentic AI in manual RFP and bidding processes to reduce repetitive tasks,' 'automation and AI adoption considered key to staying updated,' and confirmed Sierra Gorda is piloting Microsoft Copilot in two internal projects. He explicitly asked how Zycus could deliver agentic automation for bid evaluation and supplier workflows. This is a clear AI Hungry signal: the buyer named agentic AI as a top-two priority (alongside supplier mgmt) and is already experimenting with AI internally. However, AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are all null in Salesforce, so no formal AI excitement scoring has been logged. The call evidence shows Alberto is AI Hungry and Merlin/agentic positioning should be the centerpiece of re-engagement, but the deal's 225-day dormancy suggests the AI narrative was not activated or the business case/urgency was insufficient to convert interest into action.

deal_movement.summary: Deal created 29 Oct 2025 with immediate discovery call same day. Second discovery 29 Oct committed to follow-up demo 5 Nov. Demo cancelled. Placeholder 13 Nov was Zycus-internal (no buyer). Rep attempted three follow-ups (13 Feb, 1 Apr, 22 May 2026 per Next_Step__c) with zero buyer response. Deal has been cold for 225 days. No stage progression, no field updates beyond Next_Step logs. Last verified buyer engagement 13 Nov 2025.

items.detail: Sandra Montiel (VP Supply Chain, Decision Maker) has never engaged across 8 months. The deal is stuck at Evaluator level (Alberto) with no C-suite sponsorship or visibility. Without EB alignment, this cannot progress to commercial or close.

Alberto deferred budget clarity twice (1 Apr: 'should know more week of 6 Apr'; 22 May: 'know where budgets stand early June') and never confirmed outcome. No commercial discussion has occurred. Budget availability and FY allocation for this initiative are completely unknown.

Deal created 29 Oct 2025, close date 25 Sep 2026 (91 days out), but buyer has been silent for 225 days (since 13 Nov 2025). Stage=Qualified with no progression. The timeline is a placeholder with no validated buyer urgency or compelling event to anchor it.

Build vs. buy risk: Alberto is piloting Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot internally and called it 'more flexible and cheaper.' Sierra Gorda may conclude they can build supplier/RFP automation themselves on Power Platform rather than buy Zycus, especially given 225-day silence and no demo delivered to prove Zycus superiority.

Single-thread dependency: Alberto is the only contact and has gone silent. No multi-threading to Sandra (VP), Rodrigo (IT), or any other stakeholder. If Alberto has deprioritized or left, the deal is dead.

The deal requires immediate multi-threading to Sandra and potentially Rodrigo Ledezma (IT) to establish a true champion with executive access and budget authority.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is cooling and at extreme risk. Alberto Kunstmann, the sole engaged contact, has been silent for 225 days despite three rep follow-ups (Feb, Apr, May 2026). He cancelled the 5 Nov demo and never rescheduled. Sandra Montiel (VP, Decision Maker) has never engaged. No stage progression, no field updates, no buyer activity since 13 Nov 2025. The only 'movement' is rep-initiated outreach with no response. This is a cold, dormant deal requiring immediate revival or disqualification.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 29 Oct 2025, qualified same day. Last buyer activity 13 Nov 2025 (225 days ago). Close date 25 Sep 2026 (91 days out). Zero buyer engagement in 7+ months despite three rep-initiated follow-up attempts (13 Feb, 1 Apr, 22 May 2026 per Next_Step__c). Stage=Qualified with no progression to Formal Evaluation or beyond. Time dark (225d) exceeds entire deal age (240d) by a wide margin. No field history movement on stage, amount, or forecast since creation. This is a dormant deal requiring immediate revival or disqualification.

north_star_verdict.headline: Deal remains dark for 225 days with zero buyer engagement since November 2025 despite three documented follow-up attempts. Stuck at Qualified stage for seven months with no progression. Buyer has effectively ghosted; requires immediate re-engagement or disqualification.

flags: Single-thread risk: Alberto Kunstmann (Evaluator) is the only engaged contact and has gone silent for 225 days. Sandra Montiel (VP Supply Chain, Decision Maker) has never engaged. Multi-thread immediately to Sandra and to the relevant stakeholder (IT/Digital Transformation) to validate whether this initiative is alive and has executive sponsorship.

Economic Buyer unmapped and unengaged: Sandra Montiel controls the commercial decision but has never joined a call or been part of the conversation. The deal cannot close without direct EB access, a pricing discussion, and confirmation this is a budgeted 2026 initiative (not a 2027 exploratory effort). Escalate through the owner's manager (Alexa Bradley) to secure an executive-level introduction to Sandra and anchor the business case to her FY priorities.
best_practice_check.summary: Deal is off track and requires immediate corrective action. Primary risks: 225-day buyer silence, single-thread dependency on a cooling Evaluator-level contact, unmapped Economic Buyer, and no quantified business case to justify budget allocation. The deal will not progress without multi-threading to Sandra Montiel (VP, the real Decision Maker) and converting qualitative pain into a board-ready ROI case.

competitive_position.summary: Sabariva is the incumbent RFP/sourcing tool and the direct displacement target. Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot represents an internal build alternative (do-it-yourself) that Alberto is actively piloting, making it a live competitive threat given Sierra Gorda's heavy Microsoft investment and cost-consciousness. However, neither Sabariva nor the DIY approach can deliver enterprise-grade agentic AI for bid evaluation, supplier onboarding automation, or risk mapping at the scale Alberto articulated needing. The HIGHEST current threat is do-nothing/status quo, evidenced by 225 days of buyer silence and deal dormancy. No other named vendors (Coupa, Ariba, Jaeger) are confirmed active competitors—Alberto acknowledged them as market peers but provided no evidence of active evaluation. Recency: all competitive intel is from 29 Oct 2025; no updates in 8 months. Zycus has not yet demonstrated the Merlin agentic wedge in a live session, so the competitive differentiation remains theoretical.

competitors.how_we_win: Re-engage Alberto and escalate to Sandra Montiel (VP) with a board-level business case quantifying manual-process cost and Merlin ROI; create urgency by tying to FY budget cycle or competitive-market risk (peer mines automating faster)

competitors.name: Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot (internal build)

competitors.how_we_win: Concede Power Platform wins for lightweight internal workflow automation; position Zycus Merlin as the enterprise-grade supplier-facing and agentic-AI layer (bid analysis, supplier risk scoring, onboarding orchestration) that Power Platform cannot deliver at scale. Highlight integration: Zycus embeds INTO Teams (their future interface), does not replace their Microsoft investment. Prove faster time-to-value vs. custom development and lower total-cost-of-ownership across a 3-year horizon.

competitors.name: Sabariva
competitors.quote: Alberto: 'Today use Sabariva to do our business and negotiation and all the stage of RFP, invite supplier, receive quotations, creating the comparative box, and then evaluate. This stage is very manual today, and we are open to investigate new features to replace what we have today.'

competitors.how_we_win: Sabariva acknowledged as inadequate (manual, no AI, no supplier-relationship features). Displacement case is strong IF Zycus can prove Merlin agentic bid-analysis and supplier-onboarding automation deliver measurable cycle-time reduction and cost savings vs. Sabariva's static workflows. Must quantify the pain of manual quotation comparison Alberto described.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Alberto explicitly stated interest in 'agentic AI' to automate manual RFP/bid evaluation workflows and emphasized that 'automation and AI adoption are key to staying updated' (29 Oct 2025). He is piloting Microsoft Copilot internally, signaling AI appetite and willingness to adopt AI-driven tools. However, Zycus has NOT yet demonstrated Merlin agentic AI capabilities in a live session—the 5 Nov demo was cancelled and never rescheduled—so the AI positioning is theoretical only. Products__c lists Merlin, Merlin Intake, Agentic AI, and ANA, confirming the opportunity is scoped for AI/Merlin, but the buyer has not seen it in action. AI positioning is under-delivered relative to the buyer's stated interest: Alberto wanted to see how Zycus agentic AI could replace Sabariva's manual workflows, and Zycus has not yet shown him. Score: Medium potential, under-positioned in execution.

items.quote: Alberto: 'We have JD Edwards as ERP. Procurement tools will be integrated into Microsoft Teams replacing Webex.' Kevin confirmed Zycus integrates into Teams.
items.criterion: Integration with JD Edwards ERP, Microsoft Power Platform, Microsoft Teams

items.quote: Alberto: 'Microsoft Power Platform is more flexible and cheaper in my opinion.' This signals price sensitivity and preference for lower-cost alternatives, a potential risk if Zycus pricing is not competitive vs. DIY/Power Platform.
items.criterion: Cost-conscious, prefer flexible and cheaper solutions

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Alberto articulated clear capability requirements on 29 Oct 2025: supplier relationship management (onboarding, risk mapping, development), agentic AI for manual RFP/bid evaluation automation, and integration into Microsoft Teams. Zycus product scope (iSource, iSupplier, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, ANA per Products__c) directly maps to these needs. Fit appears strong on paper, but it is entirely unvalidated—no demo delivered, no use-case workshop, no live Merlin agentic-AI proof point shown. Alberto's silence for 225 days suggests either the fit was weaker than the discovery suggested, the business case/urgency was insufficient, or an internal decision (budget, priority, Power Platform pivot) deprioritized the initiative. Fit confidence is low until re-engaged and validated in a live session.

hard.ais_why:
• Customer already runs two active pilots involving MS Copilot for internal solutions, signaling real commitment to AI-driven optimization.
• Stated needs include enhanced AI-driven optimization as an ongoing priority and embedding AI/automation in manual RFP, bidding, and evaluation workflows.
• Next step is to engage Alberto Kunstmann to gauge interest in ANA and Intake. Both modules explicitly named for follow-up.

packets.subject: Do-nothing / Status Quo

value.name: Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot (internal build)
value.quote: Alberto: 'We are implementing Microsoft Power Platform, developing solutions internally with consulting; piloting Copilot in two internal projects. It's more flexible and cheaper in my opinion.'
value.source: Avoma discovery call 29 Oct 2025, Alberto Kunstmann
value.how_we_win: Concede Power Platform wins for lightweight internal workflow automation; position Zycus Merlin as the enterprise-grade supplier-facing and agentic-AI layer (bid analysis, supplier risk scoring, onboarding orchestration) that Power Platform cannot deliver at scale. Highlight integration: Zycus embeds INTO Teams (their future interface), does not replace their Microsoft investment. Prove faster time-to-value vs. custom development and lower total-cost-of-ownership across a 3-year horizon.

packets.subject: Microsoft Power Platform + Copilot (internal build)

value.name: Sabariva
value.quote: Alberto: 'Today use Sabariva to do our business and negotiation and all the stage of RFP, invite supplier, receive quotations, creating the comparative box, and then evaluate. This stage is very manual today, and we are open to investigate new features to replace what we have today.'
value.source: Avoma discovery call 29 Oct 2025, Alberto Kunstmann
value.how_we_win: Sabariva acknowledged as inadequate (manual, no AI, no supplier-relationship features). Displacement case is strong IF Zycus can prove Merlin agentic bid-analysis and supplier-onboarding automation deliver measurable cycle-time reduction and cost savings vs. Sabariva's static workflows. Must quantify the pain of manual quotation comparison Alberto described.

packets.subject: Sabariva
